path_c_phase1_logistic_results.parquet.note: per-N predicted P(drift); coefficients/AUCs/importances in this run_summary

univariate_strong_cells_auc_gt_0.60.example: ATP_MAIN r45_54 gap>=5: base 12.3% -> 40.0% fill, lift 3.24x, AUC 0.746

multivariate.note: taker_imbalance dominates (Plex predicted) but with NEGATIVE sign - net buying pushes price up, away from a below-anchor bid (mechanically coherent for drift_reached_bid).

conditional_rules_vs_v3.interpretation: Both binary skip-gates LOWER ROI than place-everywhere v3, because v3 already falls back to a costless taker-at-anchor on misses, so skipping only forgoes profitable fills (reduces volume, no offsetting gain). The predictor's value is OFFSET MODULATION (aggressiveness ~ P(drift)), not binary skip-gating - that is Phase 2.

validation_gates.gate2_feature_non_degeneracy: PASS - all 3 features vary in 36/36 cells.
validation_gates.gate3_drift_count_match: PASS exact (3,983 == Path B v3 fill count).
validation_gates.gate5_conditional_rule_sanity: DOCUMENTED TRADEOFF (not STOP): Rule A 9.17% / Rule B 9.36% < v3 12.11%. Binary skip-gating reduces volume without increasing per-fill capture; the signal must be deployed as offset modulation (Phase 2), not skip-gating.

recommendation: BUILD Path C Phase 2 - as OFFSET MODULATION (P(drift) -> bid aggressiveness), NOT skip-gating. Predictor has real out-of-sample signal (holdout AUC 0.73); paired_arb_gap (within-cell lift up to 3.5x) is the strongest deployable feature; taker_imbalance dominates the model (negative sign). If Phase 2's modulated-offset sim doesn't beat v3's +3.41pp, the Path B ceiling is near the feature set's extractable limit.
deviations: Feature 2 sourced from per_minute_features (pre-T-4h window [240,270]) not premarket_tape_v1 (which lacks pre-T-4h data); causally-correct before-window. taker_data_available 61.1% (sparse premarket flow).
No sklearn on VPS -> AUC/logistic(IRLS)/CPCV/importance implemented in pure numpy.
Tournament block = ISO year-week of the event date (per the prompt's week-of-year proxy).

pred_p_drift written to outputs is from the holdout-trained model applied to all N (in-sample for CPCV rows; use CPCV/holdout AUCs for signal-strength claims). The conditional-rule comparison uses it in-sample-optimistically yet rules still underperform v3, so the skip-gating conclusion is robust.
